name: Aranayake Landslide
description: One of Sri Lanka's deadliest landslides, triggered by intense monsoon rainfall. A massive slope failure buried parts of Siripura and Pallebage villages in Aranayake, Kegalle. Over 100 bodies were recovered and hundreds of homes were destroyed. The military led a large-scale rescue operation for days.

name: Koslanda Tea Estate Landslide
description: A catastrophic landslide struck the Meeriyabedda Tea Estate in Koslanda, Badulla district during heavy seasonal rains. Lines of tea plantation workers' quarters were buried under tonnes of debris. The tragedy led to increased scrutiny of plantation-zone disaster preparedness across the central hills.

name: Ratnapura Landslide Cluster
description: Southwest monsoon rainfall caused multiple slope failures across Ratnapura and Kegalle districts. Several villages and access roads were cut off. Tens of thousands were displaced, and the DMC issued red-level warnings for the entire Sabaragamuwa province.

name: Nuwara Eliya Hill Collapse
description: Prolonged rainfall over the central highlands destabilized slopes in Nuwara Eliya district. Several hillside residences and tea estate divisions were engulfed. The A7 highway was blocked for several days disrupting supply chains from the hill country.

name: Galle–Matara Road Landslides
description: A series of roadside landslides along the southern coast's hilly interior disrupted transport between Galle and Matara. Multiple cuts on the Akuressa–Deniyaya road left villages isolated. Army sappers were deployed to clear debris and restore access.

name: Northeast Monsoon Kegalle Slides
description: The northeast inter-monsoon brought flash landslides to the hill margins of Kegalle and Kandy. Early warning systems issued alerts, enabling partial evacuations. Despite this, fatalities occurred in areas with difficult terrain access, underlining the need for last-mile communication.

name: Badulla District Slides
description: Moderate rainfall triggered a series of earthflows and shallow slides across Badulla and Monaragala. Agricultural land and tea estates suffered significant losses. Infrastructure damage included road cuts and bridge washouts.

name: Matale Central Hills Slide
description: The southwest monsoon's onset caused slope failures in the central highlands affecting Matale and Kandy outskirts. The Kandy–Dambulla A9 highway was temporarily blocked. Damage to paddy fields and home gardens was significant.

name: Kalutara–Ratnapura Border Slides
description: Early southwest monsoon rainfall triggered debris flows along the Kalutara–Ratnapura border zone. Homes on steep slopes in Palindanuwara were evacuated. The NBRO issued red warnings for 6 districts as conditions remained unstable.

name: Ratnapura Kalawana Slides
description: Exceptional 24-hour rainfall totals triggered flash landslides in the Kalawana and Nivithigala divisions of Ratnapura. Hundreds of families were evacuated to temporary shelters. NBRO teams and the army conducted search and rescue operations over 72 hours.

name: Haldummulla Slide
description: A significant landslide struck Haldummulla in the Uva province's Badulla district following sustained northeast monsoon rainfall. A section of the Haputale–Koslanda road was destroyed, isolating several villages for days.

name: Meeriyabedda–Welimada Slides
description: Inter-monsoonal rains triggered a series of landslides across the central highland districts. Tea and vegetable cultivation areas suffered major losses. The National Building Research Organisation (NBRO) conducted an urgent assessment of vulnerable settlements.
